w:r.w:t: CONDITIONALLY SAFE — Fix issues before client delivery
Electron + Vanilla JS + SQLite (sql.js) — Offline Desktop App
3 HIGH — must fix before going live
Transactions, duplicate checks, roundMoney(), error logging

The good news: this code is significantly better than a typical vibe-coded project. It has proper SQL transactions on all critical operations, uses parameterized queries (no SQL injection), has duplicate roll number prevention, and a roundMoney() function for financial precision. However, there are 3 HIGH severity issues that WILL cause problems for your client if not fixed first.
These are solid practices found in your code — do not remove them:
Used properly in create-student, checkout-cart, promote-students. This prevents partial saves if something fails midway.
All SQL uses ? placeholders (e.g. run('SELECT ... WHERE id=?', [id])). This completely prevents SQL injection attacks.
Financial calculations use Math.round with EPSILON — this prevents floating point errors like 0.1+0.2=0.30000000004.
create-student checks for existing roll_no before inserting.
The system checks if a fee already exists for the month before generating — prevents duplicate monthly fees.
logError() appends to error_logs.txt in AppData — your client can send you logs if something breaks.
The checkout button is disabled during processing (btn.disabled = true) — prevents double-click duplicate orders.
schema_migrations table allows upgrading existing databases without data loss.
🔴 HIGH Severity Issues (Must Fix Before Delivery)
Fee Save Button — No Double-Click Protection

The saveFee() function in fees.html has no button disable/loading state. If a user clicks 'Save' twice quickly, two identical fee entries will be created. This is your main duplication bug from GalaxyERP.
Disable the submit button at start of saveFee(), re-enable after completion. Add a flag: let isSaving = false; check it at top of function.

ipcMain.handle('update-fee') only updates: student_id, amount, status, description, type, month. It does NOT update the debit and credit columns. Editing a fee entry will silently corrupt the ledger balance — the displayed amount changes but the actual balance calculation stays wrong.
Add debit and credit to the UPDATE query: 'UPDATE fees SET student_id=?, amount=?, debit=?, credit=?, status=?, description=?, type=?, month=? WHERE id=?'

create-backup always writes to 'D:\SMS_Connect_Backups'. If the client's computer has no D: drive (many laptops only have C:), backup silently fails. This means the client thinks they have a backup but they don't — potential data loss.
Use dialog.showSaveDialog() to let user pick location, OR default to Desktop/Documents path using app.getPath('desktop') or app.getPath('documents').
🟡 MEDIUM Severity Issues (Fix Soon After Delivery)
Bulk Fee Apply — No Double-Click Protection
When applying fee to a whole class, saveFee() runs Promise.all() on all students. No button is disabled. Double-clicking adds fees to every student in the class twice — hard to reverse.
Same fix as above: disable button, set isSubmitting flag, re-enable in finally block.

There are TWO checkout handlers: checkout-inventory (single item) and checkout-cart (cart). checkout-inventory gets the last inserted sale ID with 'SELECT id FROM sales ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1' — if two sales happen at nearly the same time, it could link the fee entry to the wrong sale.
Use the result of db.exec('SELECT last_insert_rowid()') immediately after the INSERT, inside the same transaction — not a separate SELECT query.
Bank Stats — String Concatenation in SQL

get-bank-stats builds SQL by concatenating the 'period' variable directly into the query string (template literals with ${where}). Although period is validated by if/else checks, this pattern is risky and differs from your safe parameterized approach everywhere else.
Not exploitable in current form, but refactor to use the same parameterized style for consistency and safety.

Login uses SHA-256 for password hashing. SHA-256 is fast — attackers can crack it with GPU in seconds. For an offline app this is lower risk, but if the database file is ever copied or stolen, passwords are easily cracked.
Use bcrypt or Argon2 (both available as npm packages in Electron). Even a work factor of 10 makes brute force practically impossible.
Editing a Student's Monthly Fee Doesn't Re-generate Current Month Fee

update-student just updates the student record. If an admin changes a student's monthly fee from Rs. 1500 to Rs. 2000, the current month's already-generated fee entry stays at 1500. No automatic adjustment.
After update-student, check if the current month's fee exists and update its amount, or show a notice to the admin.

reset-database deletes students, teachers, fees, etc., but does NOT clear last_fee_generation_month from settings. After a reset, monthly fees won't regenerate because the system thinks it already ran for this month.
Add settings cleanup to reset: run("DELETE FROM settings WHERE key = 'last_fee_generation_month'").

delete-student permanently removes the student AND orphans all their fee history, attendance records, and exam results (no CASCADE or cleanup). The client could accidentally delete a student and lose all their records.
Already have 'status' column — use status='deleted' (soft delete) instead of DELETE. Add a filter to hide deleted students from lists but keep their history intact.
Fix 1: Fee Save Button — Double-Click Protection
Replace the start of your saveFee() function in fees.html with this:
let isSavingFee = false; // Add this at top of script
try { ... your existing code ... } finally { isSavingFee = false; }
Fix 2: update-fee in main.js — Add Debit/Credit

ipcMain.handle('update-fee', (e, d) => run('UPDATE fees SET student_id=?, amount=?, status=?, description=?, type=?, month=? WHERE id=?', [d.student_id, d.amount, d.status, d.description, d.type, d.month, d.id]));

ipcMain.handle('update-fee', (e, d) => run('UPDATE fees SET student_id=?, amount=?, debit=?, credit=?, status=?, description=?, type=?, month=? WHERE id=?', [d.student_id, d.amount, Number(d.debit)||0, Number(d.credit)||0, d.status, d.description, d.type, d.month, d.id]));
Replace the backup path logic in create-backup with:
Fix 4: Reset Database — Clear Fee Month Setting
Add this line inside reset-database, after deleting tables:
Complete all items before handing the software to your client:
— use Desktop or Documents, not D:\\
Test: Add a student, pay fee, verify ledger balance is correct
Test: Add fee twice quickly — confirm only one entry saved
Test: Run backup — verify file appears on Desktop
Test: Reset database — add new student — verify fee generates for this month
Test: Edit a fee entry — verify the ledger balance updates correctly
Summary — Will Client Face GalaxyERP Issues Again?
Comparison with common vibe-coded ERP problems:
Checks exist() before inserting monthly fee
Atomic sequential invoice numbers in settings table
BEGIN TRANSACTION used on all multi-step operations
Good error handling, needs minor improvements
CONCLUSION: Make the 3 HIGH fixes (1-2 hours of work), run the checklist tests, and this project is ready for client delivery. The core architecture is sound.
